title: Establish doctrine: every non-trivial bead closure cites a PatrolReceipt
description:
The 2026-05-14 cadence-bead-corridor-staleness investigation surfaced a doctrine gap: bead closures use a prose `resolutionNote` field, which is a weak receipt — it asserts the bead is done but doesn't point at typed evidence that can be re-verified. The substrate already has the typed shape (`PatrolReceipt` from receipt-schemas v0.1.0 in core-entity-set-v1.0.0; binding suffix `.patrol-receipt.json`). On 2026-05-14, we demonstrated the pattern by writing 3 PatrolReceipts (prime-tempo-commission, core-entity-migrator-chain-completeness, cadence-bead-corridor-staleness) and retroactively adding a `closureReceiptRef` field to the 3 closed beads pointing at the receipts. Doctrine proposal: every non-trivial bead closure cites a `closureReceiptRef`. Trivial closures (single-line tidy-up, no audit/investigation) can keep just `resolutionNote`. The cutoff between trivial and non-trivial needs operator definition.

Open questions for the doctrine:
1. Where do receipts live? This session placed them under `<agent-home>/agenda/receipts/`. Should that path be canonical?
2. PatrolReceipt has koma-shape (digikomaSlug, digikomaVersion). Manual claude investigations stretch the semantics. Do we need a non-koma receipt shape, or is the stretch acceptable with explicit `extensions.manualInvestigation: true`?
3. Should `closureReceiptRef` be a new optional field on the bead schema, or stay informal via the resolutionNote prose?
4. Should receipts be required for bead CREATION too (i.e., 'evidenced opens' as well as 'evidenced closes')?
